tool_args.label: Quest Diagnostics Drug & Alcohol Testing Services Contract

payload.project_name: Quest Diagnostics Drug & Alcohol Testing Services Contract
payload.description: Award of a competitively negotiated miscellaneous service contract to Quest Diagnostics Clinical Laboratories Inc. to provide Drug & Alcohol Testing Services in support of MTA pre- and post-hire workplace drug/alcohol testing policies for the MTA agencies. Contract term April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2030, not-to-exceed amount of $9,535,161.
payload.implementing_agency: MTA Division of Safety - Office of Occupational Health Services

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

payload.description: Clinical Reference Laboratory Best and Final Offer for the five-year drug/alcohol testing contract
Labcorp Employer Services Inc. Best and Final Offer for the five-year drug/alcohol testing contract
Initial five-year cost proposals from three short-listed firms (CRL, LabCorp, Quest) excluding oral fluid testing costs
Number of OHS specimen collection sites across the MTA agencies
Period of performance for the Quest Diagnostics Drug & Alcohol Testing Services contract
payload.event_name: Quest Diagnostics Drug & Alcohol Testing Services Contract Term

payload.description: The current contract with Quest will expire on May 31, 2025
DOT lab-based oral fluid pricing became available and CRL, LabCorp, and Quest were requested to provide Best and Final Offers in December 2024

tool_args.label: Oral-fluid testing detects more recent drug use

payload.claim_text: Lab-based oral fluid testing will increase detection of more recent drug use (as early as one hour following use, whereas use in urine is generally not detected within 24 hours following use).
payload.subject: lab-based oral fluid testing
payload.description: Benefit of oral-fluid testing: detection of drug use as early as one hour after use

payload.claim_text: Lab-based oral fluid testing will provide for continuous 'direct observation' of an employee by the OHS technician, meaning private use of a bathroom where specimen tampering could occur will be significantly reduced.
payload.subject: lab-based oral fluid testing
payload.description: Benefit of oral-fluid testing: direct observation eliminates privacy-related specimen tampering

tool_args.label: Oral-fluid testing eliminates shy bladder events

payload.claim_text: Lab-based oral fluid testing will provide for practical elimination of 'shy bladder' events when an employee is unable to provide sufficient urine specimen at the start of the collection process that would then necessitate the employee to be afforded up to a three-hour waiting period to consume MTA-provided water to assist in the collection of a sufficient amount of urine.
payload.subject: lab-based oral fluid testing
payload.description: Benefit of oral-fluid testing: eliminates shy bladder events and reduces time spent at OHS collection sites

tool_args.label: OHS Drug and Alcohol Programs Selection Committee

payload.entity_name: OHS Drug and Alcohol Programs Selection Committee
payload.description: Selection Committee consisting of OHS Senior Director of Drug and Alcohol Programs, Director of Testing and Laboratory Services, and Director of Information Management and Reporting

tool_args.label: Carl Hamann, Acting Chief Safety Officer
MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights

payload.entity_name: MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights

payload.description: MTA division that houses the Office of Occupational Health Services

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

tool_args.label: SC unanimously determined Quest highest ranked

payload.claim_text: The SC unanimously determined Quest to be the highest ranked proposer offering the best value proposal to MTA taking into consideration technical and cost evaluation criteria in the RFP.
payload.subject: Selection Committee
payload.description: Selection Committee unanimously determined Quest as highest ranked proposer offering best value

payload.claim_text: The MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights (DDCR) has established zero percent MWBE/SDVOB goals on this contract because the participant data and benefit contract provisions are considered highly sensitive and confidential in nature. Subcontracting to third parties would increase the risk and exposure of confidential information.
payload.subject: MWBE/SDVOB goals
payload.description: Zero percent MWBE/SDVOB goals due to confidentiality of participant data and benefit contract provisions

tool_args.label: Federally mandated drug testing governed by FTA and FRA

payload.claim_text: MTA's federally mandated drug testing Policies are specifically governed by regulations defined by the Federal Transit Administration and the Federal Railroad Administration under the federal Department of Transportation (DOT). This includes DOT's rule: Title 49 CFR Part 40, 'Procedures for Transportation Workplace Drug and Alcohol Testing Programs.'
payload.subject: MTA's federally mandated drug testing policies
payload.description: Federally mandated drug testing governed by FTA and FRA under DOT regulations including 49 CFR Part 40

tool_args.label: New contract includes electronic paperless testing

payload.claim_text: The new contract will also include the ability to replace paper-based documentation of each testing event with a fully electronic process, as well as automated scheduling of donors at lab-affiliated collection sites across the NYC metropolitan region.
payload.subject: new Quest contract
payload.description: New contract introduces fully electronic documentation and automated donor scheduling

tool_args.label: Quest contract has timeline event: contract term

payload.description: The Quest Diagnostics Drug & Alcohol Testing Services Contract has a contract term of April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2030

tool_args.label: Quest contract has timeline event: current contract expiry

payload.description: The current contract with Quest will expire on May 31, 2025

tool_args.label: Quest contract has timeline event: BAFO submissions

payload.description: BAFO submissions occurred in December 2024 for the Quest Diagnostics contract procurement
The contract is awarded to Quest Diagnostics Clinical Laboratories Inc.
The contract is requested and managed by the MTA Division of Safety - Office of Occupational Health Services
The procurement document is published by the Metropolitan Transportation Authority

tool_args.label: OHS is part of Division of Safety

payload.description: The Office of Occupational Health Services is part of the MTA Division of Safety
The Quest contract project has a not-to-exceed amount of $9,535,161

payload.claim_text: Funding for services associated with this contract will be provided by operating accounts from agencies supported by OHS.
payload.subject: contract funding
payload.description: Contract funding from operating accounts of agencies supported by OHS

payload.claim_text: 1. Perform all services in-house. This alternative is not feasible as the MTA is required to contract with a laboratory that is certified by the Department of Health and Human Services. 2. Do not approve award of the contract. This alternative is not feasible as the MTA is obligated to comply with federal regulations mandating workplace drug/alcohol testing of all employees performing safety-sensitive work (approximately 45,000 MTA agency-wide) and job candidates being considered for performing safety-sensitive work.
payload.subject: contract alternatives
payload.description: Both alternatives (in-house performance and not approving the contract) were considered not feasible
